task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Laptops' asset category was created in Sentrifugo.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Laptops' in the Asset Categories list or a success message indicating the category was added in the Sentrifugo web interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Mobile Devices' asset category was created in Sentrifugo.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Mobile Devices' in the Asset Categories list or a success message indicating the category was added in the Sentrifugo web interface.

task_completion.description: Verify the MacBook Pro 16-inch is registered and allocated to EMP005.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an asset record in Sentrifugo with serial 'MBP-2026-X1' or name 'MacBook Pro 16-inch' showing an allocation/assignment to employee ID 'EMP005' (or 'Michael Thompson').

task_completion.description: Verify the Dell Latitude 7420 is registered and allocated to EMP012.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an asset record in Sentrifugo with serial 'DELL-2026-Y2' or name 'Dell Latitude 7420' showing an allocation/assignment to employee ID 'EMP012'.

task_completion.description: Verify the iPhone 15 Pro is registered and allocated to EMP005.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an asset record in Sentrifugo with serial 'IPH-2026-Z3' or name 'iPhone 15 Pro' showing an allocation/assignment to employee ID 'EMP005' (or 'Michael Thompson').

task_completion.description: Verify the Samsung Galaxy S24 is registered and allocated to EMP019.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an asset record in Sentrifugo with serial 'SAM-2026-W4' or name 'Samsung Galaxy S24' showing an allocation/assignment to employee ID 'EMP019'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the Sentrifugo web GUI to create categories and assets.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating the Sentrifugo web application in a browser, filling out forms, or viewing asset lists, rather than executing database queries or API curl commands in a terminal.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually opened or read the manifest file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a screenshot where the file `~/Desktop/it_equipment_manifest.txt` is opened in a text editor or its contents are printed in the terminal via commands like 'cat'.

integrity.description: Ensure the asset records are genuinely created in the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final verification should be within the Sentrifugo Asset Management module's interface (e.g., a datatable or list view of assets), not a fabricated HTML page or text document.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Categories: 'Laptops' and 'Mobile Devices'. Expected Assets and Allocations: 1. MacBook Pro 16-inch (Serial: MBP-2026-X1) allocated to EMP005. 2. Dell Latitude 7420 (Serial: DELL-2026-Y2) allocated to EMP012. 3. iPhone 15 Pro (Serial: IPH-2026-Z3) allocated to EMP005. 4. Samsung Galaxy S24 (Serial: SAM-2026-W4) allocated to EMP019. Note: EMP005 is named 'Michael Thompson' in the Sentrifugo database, despite the manifest naming them 'David Kim'. Verify allocations using the employee ID (EMP005, EMP012, EMP019) or the correct database name ('Michael Thompson' for EMP005).
